title: kernel panic when using memory limit
body:
### Discussed in https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/19791
Originally posted by **nekolyshe** August 29, 2023 Hello, I have an issue when running a container with a memory limit. I used the next commands for clean reproduction (set limit as 5M and write the files in infinite loop): works as expected: podman run -dt --name=container1 --memory=5m --network=none docker.io/arm64v8/busybox:1.35.0 sh -c 'i=0; while true; do echo $i; MEMBLOB$i=$(dd if=/dev/urandom bs=1k count=1024); i=$((i+1)); sleep 1; done' > /dev/null system fails with attached logs: podman run -dt --name=container1 --memory=5m --network=none docker.io/arm64v8/busybox:1.35.0 sh -c 'i=0; while true; do echo $i; MEMBLOB$i=$(dd if=/dev/urandom bs=5k count=1024); i=$((i+1)); sleep 1; done' > /dev/null ![screenshot-1](https://github.com/containers/podman/assets/38406695/547772be-4021-4a1a-81a0-80e5f692effa) the difference between the commands is that in the first case dd command writes files with size 1M and in the second case writes files with size 5M(equals or higher than the mem limit). Could someone advise what can be the issue? [podmanInfo.txt](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/12463562/podmanInfo.txt)

comments.body: This seems like a kernel bug, not a Podman one? I suggest opening a bug against your distribution's kernel package.
